frameworks.name: adaptive memory distortion
frameworks.one_line: memories of similar items are exaggerated apart to reduce mutual interference

frameworks.name: representational similarity analysis (fMRI pattern analysis)
frameworks.one_line: neural representational distance between competing memories indexed by multivariate patterns

frameworks.name: memory interference theory
frameworks.one_line: overlap between memory traces causes interference and forgetting

core_question: Are behavioral and neural memory representations adaptively distorted to reduce interference between similar memories?
key_assumptions: similarity between memories drives interference and forgetting
exaggerating subtle differences can reduce mutual interference
parietal cortex carries feature-specific memory content during recall
manipulations: subtle color differences between otherwise identical object images
whether color was diagnostic for discriminating competing memories
repeated learning and retrieval of object-color associations
key_findings: behavioral color memory exaggerates differences between similar objects
greater memory exaggeration is associated with lower memory interference
color information in parietal cortex is stronger when color discriminates competing memories
greater representational distance in parietal cortex predicts more exaggeration and less interference
interpretations: memory representations are adaptively distorted away from competitors to reduce interference
parietal cortex supports feature-level differentiation of competing memories
